metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your 3-year-old in and around Hackney! Get ready for some animal encounters, creative crafts, and outdoor adventures. I've found some fantastic free options too, making it a budget-friendly weekend of fun!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed for children, with collections of toys, design and childhood objects.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for children with lots of hands-on exhibits and space to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Bethnal Green. A short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a less crowded experience.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A real working farm in the heart of Hackney, with animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for little ones to see farm animals up close, with a relaxed and friendly atmosphere.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle along the Regent's Canal, or take a bus from Hackney Central.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities on their website.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers. This playground is small and safe, designed for the little ones.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk to the playground.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy in the surrounding park.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers
activities.whyItWorks: The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book in advance to guarantee entry.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Vast park with playgrounds, walking trails, and sporting venues.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space for running around, with playgrounds suitable for toddlers and young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk into the park.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events and activities.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore how homes and domestic life have changed over the centuries. Gardens outside.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of interesting exhibits to spark curiosity, plus the gardens are great for a stroll.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus from Hackney Road or walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for family-friendly events.

activities.title: Camley Street Natural Park
activities.description: An urban nature reserve, where you can go bug hunting.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids can explore the nature with free family-friendly nature activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to King's Cross and walk to Camley Street.
transport.tip: Perfect for a spring day.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Iconic museum with dinosaur exhibits, interactive displays, and fascinating collections.
activities.whyItWorks: Endless fascination for kids, especially the dinosaurs! Plenty of space to move around and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Piccadilly Line) to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science, technology, engineering and mathematics.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on displays that engage young minds, with the Wonderlab offering a particularly immersive experience (ticketed).

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Piccadilly Line) to South Kensington.
transport.tip: The Garden in the basement is a great interactive space for young children.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm in Zone 1, with goats, sheep and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It is a must for families and animal-lovers, with a host of goats, sheep and smaller animals right on your doorstep.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria line to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Check for Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Completely free, with wallabies, lemurs and Scottish wildcats.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Bring a picnic to enjoy in the park.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, with playgrounds, sand pits, a paddling pool (seasonal) and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Adventure playground with aerial slide, paddling pool and city farm.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Russell Square.
transport.tip: Perfect for a summer day.

activities.title: Crystal Palace Park Dinosaurs
activities.description: Park with wooden carved dinosaurs in the play area, a maze and lakes.
activities.whyItWorks: Fantastic place for little dinosaur lovers to explore, with dinosaurs to climb on in the play area, a maze and lakes too.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Crystal Palace.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic.

activities.title: Holland Park Adventure Playground
activities.description: Playground aimed at older kids, but toddlers enjoy it too.
activities.whyItWorks: Has lots of sand and is beloved by children

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Holland Park.
transport.tip: The playground is aimed at older kids.

activities.title: Southbank Centre Family Activities
activities.description: Riverside hub of arts, fun and food—perfect for families. Often has free installations.
activities.whyItWorks: With kids' concerts, festivals, creative workshops, hands-on installations and space to scoot or picnic, it's welcoming, accessible and packed with energy

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Jubilee Line) to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the website for free events.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Ham House and Garden
activities.description: Easter trail inspired by the British Civil Wars, with activities for the whole family. Includes trail sheet, bunny ears and a chocolate egg.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration, fun activities and a chocolate treat at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Richmond, then a bus to Ham House.
transport.tip: Book in advance, as it's a popular event.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.address: Hampton Court, East Molesey, Surrey KT8 9AU
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration, a fun search, and a chocolate reward.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Easter Egg trail, animal encounters and play areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Indoor and outdoor adventure play, animals and Easter fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hatton Cross then a bus.
transport.tip: Book online for a cheaper ticket.

activities.title: London Zoo Zoo-normous Easter Hunt
activities.description: Follow trails to discover hidden eggs while learning fun facts about wildlife.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a zoo visit with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail at Walthamstow Wetlands
activities.description: Discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet
activities.whyItWorks: Children aged 8 and under are invited to discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Walthamstow Wetlands.
transport.tip: Perfect for a spring day.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: A musical adaptation of the classic Paddington Bear stories.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and heartwarming show for the whole family, based on a beloved character.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (various lines) to Charing Cross or Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend performances.

activities.title: Eltham Palace and Gardens Easter Quest
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun outdoor activities and a chocolate.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Eltham.
transport.tip: Remember to book in advance.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: A vibrant and visually stunning musical adaptation of the classic Disney film.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic show that captivates audiences of all ages with its music, costumes and story.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Piccadilly Line) to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book in advance for best seats and prices.
